explanatory_strength.rationale: The levered-stub mechanism remains specific: FY2025 operating cash flow ($3.1B, ~$4.70/sh) must service ~$29B debt and leases while capex ($3.8B) exceeds GAAP free cash flow, and proof-first components separate network cash, net financial claims, and a cycle reserve. Round-two research response confirms the distinguishing credit test—whether OCF can deleverage before refinancing stress—is named but still lacks a year-by-year maturity, interest-coverage, or covenant bridge; Lawrence 27% paths remain earnings sensitivities, not credit-survival math.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Balance-sheet anchors reconcile from FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q (cash $902M, total debt and leases $29,007M, OCF $3,099M, diluted shares ~658.6M) and all four valuation-proof rows remain mechanically valid. The targeted research loop explicitly requested year-by-year maturities, interest expense, weighted-average coupon, and covenant headroom; research_response status is unresolved—the frozen packet cannot extract InterestExpense, maturity tables beyond the $3,753M current bucket, revolver availability, or covenant terms. management_facts has zero claims; AAdvantage success_probability remains null. Decision-critical credit inputs required for this persona are absent.

downside_control.rationale: Net financial claims filing-locked at −$42.67/sh and cycle_leverage_reserve at −$9/sh acknowledge fuel, recession, and leverage stress, but neither ties to interest burden or maturity concentration. Equity downside is unbounded: dhando none, component low sums to −$41.70/sh (−375% to low), and Lawrence bear (15.2%) is not a credit-default path. Thin cash ($902M) against $3.75B current maturities plus capex exceeding OCF leaves dilutive refinancing and liquidity exhaustion plausible and unquantified after the failed research loop.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Proof-first component base economic value remains −$9.79/sh at $15.14, so contract base annualized return is null; high component case reaches 8.12% annualized, below the 12% credit-cycle hurdle. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% and bear 15.2% assume mid-cycle owner-cash growth without credit-stressed equity math. Round-two confirmation that credit-bridge inputs are unavailable prevents certifying any return path that dominates alternatives on a financing-cycle basis.

claims.claim: American Airlines remains squarely in the credit-cycle power zone: ~$28.1B net debt (filing-locked), $902M cash, FY2025 operating income down 44% YoY to $1.5B, and capex exceeding OCF—equity is a levered stub whose value depends on refinancing access, not base-case earnings alone.

Round-two research loop confirmed the packet cannot supply year-by-year debt and finance-lease maturities, annual interest expense, weighted-average coupon, or covenant headroom reconciled to the FY2025 10-K debt footnote; without these inputs a credit-tightening stress case on equity recovery versus dilution cannot be run.

All four additive proof rows still reconcile mechanically (midcycle_passenger_network $33.88, aadvantage_co_brand_option $8.00, net_financial_claims −$42.67, cycle_leverage_reserve −$9.00; sum −$9.79/sh) with unique overlap keys, but the credit reserve is judgment-only and the market's deleveraging optionality at $15 cannot be certified until maturity and coverage data reconcile.

strongest_counter_explanation: Credit markets remain open for investment-grade airline issuers, Q1 2026 net income of $382M shows operating recovery, and sustained $3B+ operating cash flow can roll the ~$3.8B current debt wall while modestly paying down the $29B stack; AAdvantage co-brand economics lift normalized owner cash above depressed GAAP earnings, making $15 equity a cheap call on orderly deleveraging rather than a distressed stub.
most_important_missing_fact: Year-by-year debt and finance-lease maturity schedule with annual interest expense, weighted-average coupon, and covenant headroom reconciled to the FY2025 10-K debt footnote, so a credit-tightening stress case can be run on equity recovery versus dilution.
falsifiers: Total debt rises above $32B without proportional operating income recovery for two consecutive quarters.
Trailing four-quarter operating cash flow per share falls below $3.50 while current maturities exceed unrestricted cash plus revolver availability.
Any 12-month window where interest coverage on consolidated EBIT falls below 1.5× without a disclosed liquidity backstop.
specialist_findings: Round-two audit after targeted research loop (status unresolved). Economic claim and all four valuation-proof rows unchanged and valid. midcycle_passenger_network: FY2025 OCF ($4.70/sh) × judgment capitalization multiple; capex ($3.8B) exceeds OCF with no maintenance-capex bridge. aadvantage_co_brand_option: base $8/sh pure judgment ($5.3B milestone / 658.6M shares), null success_probability, no standalone SEC disclosure. net_financial_claims filing-locked: ($902M − $29,007M) / 658.6M ≈ −$42.67/sh; low/high only ±5% debt / +15% cash haircuts, no distressed refinancing rate. cycle_leverage_reserve (−$9/sh base) still lacks tie to interest burden or maturity concentration. Research response confirms filing_facts parses no InterestExpense or maturity table; lenses.json marks_credit_cycle key_metrics (interest_coverage, maturity_wall, net_debt_ebitda) all null. No comparables. Lawrence bear (15.2%) is earnings-path sensitivity, not credit-default stress. Contract base annualized return null; high case 8.12% fails 12% hurdle. Credit-cycle defer reaffirmed pending maturity and coverage reconciliation outside frozen packet.
